manufacturing_locations: United States (Texas, Louisiana, Iowa, Illinois, New Jersey)
dependencies: Natural gas liquids (NGLs) including ethane, propane, and butane - primarily from U.S. shale production
Crude oil-based liquids including naphtha, condensates, and gas oils
Heavy, high-sulfur crude oil for Houston refinery - historically imported from Canada, Mexico, and other global producers
Single or limited suppliers for certain raw materials and utilities specific to geographic regions
Sole dedicated sources for utilities (steam, electricity, gas) at some facilities
Propylene supplier for Brindisi, Italy site - potential shutdown risk mentioned
Water sources - concern over reliability around U.S. Gulf Coast facilities
risks: Concentration risk from limited number of suppliers for certain raw materials
Supply chain interruptions from natural disasters, water levels, marine route disruptions affecting vessels, barges, rails, trucks, and pipelines
Increased exports of NGLs and crude oil from U.S. could restrict raw material availability and increase costs
Greater restrictions on hydraulic fracturing could impact feedstock availability
Political instability in Middle East and Central/South America affecting raw material sourcing
Potential shutdown of propylene supplier impacting Brindisi, Italy operations
Water scarcity and drought conditions due to climate change could reduce fresh water availability
Transportation disruptions from hurricanes, floods, freezing conditions, tornadoes affecting U.S. Gulf Coast operations

regulatory_risks: Climate change legislation and GHG emission reduction requirements - EU Emissions Trading System (ETS) with accelerated reduction of free allowances and higher market prices
Paris Agreement implementation - national legislation and protection plans in operating jurisdictions
U.S. state-level climate regulations (Vermont, New York 'climate superfund' laws imposing strict liability on hydrocarbon companies)

EU regulatory frameworks - REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals) and CLP (Classification, Labelling and Packaging Regulation) with potential increased compliance costs, restrictions, or bans
U.S. TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act) risk evaluation process changes potentially resulting in restrictions or bans
International legally binding instrument on plastic pollution under development by UN Environment Program (target finalization 2025)
EU Circular Economy Action Plan including Single Use Plastics Directive (2019) with bans, product design requirements, extended producer responsibility, and labeling requirements
EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation replacing prior Directive
Single-use plastic bans, taxes, and Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) bills in countries and U.S. states/municipalities
Renewable Identification Numbers (RINs) requirements mandated by U.S. EPA affecting refinery profitability
Permitting requirements for operations and growth projects potentially delayed or denied
Environmental remediation obligations at current and former sites
Data privacy and security regulations in Europe, U.S., and other jurisdictions
EU Artificial Intelligence Act and other global AI legislative efforts
OECD Pillar Two Model Rules on minimum taxes implemented in UK and certain jurisdictions (effective January 1, 2024)
Potential changes to tax laws, regulations, or treaties substantially increasing tax liabilities
Trade protection measures including quotas, duties, tariffs, safeguard measures, or anti-dumping duties
Transfer pricing regulations and customs compliance requirements

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance requirements mentioned as regulatory risk
Potential for sanctions impacting operations in certain jurisdictions
Government actions including wars, terrorist activities, political instability affecting operations
Civil unrest, terrorist attacks, and demonstrations marked by violence in Middle East and South America
Political regime changes in Middle East potentially resulting in governments hostile to U.S. or Europe
Regional conflicts or civil wars spreading from current areas of unrest
